title: Functional Core, Imperative Shell, and Production Integration · Transformation Checkpoint
summary: Rebuild an unreliable data-processing service as a pure decision core with explicit operational effects. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a production-shaped functional service and decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate pure domain decisions from I/O orchestration with explicit data contracts.
Pass clocks, randomness, storage, and transport behavior through narrow callable interfaces.
Compose ingestion, validation, transformation, aggregation, and emission with bounded resource use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Functional Core Shell · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A local decision core receives an injected clock and storage callable. Replaying the same 2-event fixture must reproduce decisions while I/O failures stay at the imperative boundary. Competency probe: Separate pure domain decisions from I/O orchestration with explicit data contracts.

Which action demonstrates Functional Core Shell without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate pure domain decisions from i/o orchestration with explicit data contracts.

content.title: Evidence defense
